channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Trees within the gated Quex Road Playground area directly behind the wall bordering 26 Quex Road NW6, 27th May
item.description:
Location of the problem: Trees within the gated Quex Road Playground area directly behind the wall bordering 26 Quex Road NW6 Explain what’s wrong: The trees within the Quex Road Playground area have become heavily overgrown and are now affecting 26 Quex Road. Branches are getting increasingly close to the windows and balcony areas and are significantly blocking natural light. There is excessive sticky sap and increased bird activity making it difficult to open windows and use the outside space comfortably. The roots and overgrowth also appear to be contributing to damage and pressure on the boundary wall. Please can the trees be inspected and properly pruned and maintained before the issue worsens further.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Quex Road, Kilburn, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A panel broken on bus shelter, 27th May
item.description:
A panel has been broken on the bus shelter in William Barefoot Drive/Partridge green towards Eltham

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): William Barefoot Drive, Coldharbour, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Damage - smashed glass or panels / jagged metal edges on shelter

item.title: Rear Garden of 15 Gaymead, 27th May
item.description:
Customer lives in a council property, she has a dead tree in her gardens that's needs removing. The garden is at the rear of the property, Please can you investigate and arrange for the removal of the tree.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bolton Road, South Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 5th FLOOR KEYNES COURT, 27th May
item.description:
Any chance of a deep clean on the landing / walkways of Keynes Court 5th floor Plaster from building work carried out weeks ago grease out side first flat we have to walk through every day (and needs a good sleep through more often

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Curtis Way, Thamesmead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pipes/guttering dumped outside 7 Heathland Road N16, 27th May
item.description:
Pipes dumped outside 7 Heathland Road N16

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Heathland Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Garden tree from the woods, 27th May
item.description:
Hi, I reported an emergency as the tree from the wood snapped and was hanging down. The men come out this morning to cut the branch down, but it needs cutting back to stop this from happening again. I have small children and this could have been so dangerous if this fell down. I have attached image one where the branch has been removed but showing how much it’s hanging over. Imagine two is where it snapped. This tree really does need looking at to stop anymore branches snapping and can hurt someone. Thanks Zoe cheek

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beddington Road, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tip, 27th May
item.description:
Big fly tip on corner of King and Queen Close under the tree at the entrance. A white door and some dining chairs
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): King and Queen Close, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping and rubbish, 27th May
item.description:
I would like to report ongoing fly-tipping and rubbish being left along the public walkway at The Drive, DA8, adjacent to the rear access from Brook Street. There is a significant amount of waste materials, broken furniture, timber, bags of rubbish, construction debris and other discarded items have been left along the pathway The condition of the walkway is becoming unacceptable and is causing obstruction and environmental concerns.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 6, The Drive, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trees taking over pavement, 27th May
item.description:
At the top of Winn Road there are 2 large trees that have over grown, the lower bit of stem has loads of branches that have grown so much they over taking the pavement making it hard to stay walking in the pavement when passing near it which is forcing my partially sighted mum to swerve and go to the road which is very dangerous and I have seen many with push chairs do the same go on the road as if they are a car! While cars usually race around there from the turning ahead. An accident waiting to happen if these trees are not cut right back to clean and clear the pavement!!!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 14, Winn Road, Chinbrook, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large Mattress & Other matter flytipped in Orpington High Street, 27th May
item.description:
Next to Fiesta Mexicana restaurant in the High street opposite The White Hart pub near enough thx

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 99-101, High Street, Goddington, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
